title: Catch Policy, Injected Randomness, Immutable Collection State, and Updates · Contract Lens: Pokedex Ts Random Port
summary: Catch calls Math.random inside the handler, mutates a Map in shared state, stores transport objects, accepts duplicate names with different casing, and cannot replay outcomes. Predict the user decision, runtime value, strict type claim, promise state, API boundary, failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the pokedex ts random port increment in replayable immutable Pokemon collection reducer. Learners progress through prediction, exp…
objectives: Inject a bounded random-number port or deterministic policy and record draw evidence needed to replay a decision.
Define what catch teaches, whether chance is justified, its visible probability contract, and duplicate and failure behavior.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Pokedex Ts Random Port
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory TypeScript team handles case 1749 during a clean emitted-package smoke project. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to reconcile replayable catch decisions with immutable collection state and stable identity; the release claim must bind source, compiler, emitted JavaScript, and packed artifact; then inject one redirect, prototype-like key, terminal control, path, file, or signal failure and reject it safely. Browser code uses pure bounded runtime models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, Node readline, host streams, signals, files, package installs, handle and load claims, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Inject a bounded random-number port or deterministic policy and record draw evidence needed to replay a decision.
